rulings.Case Title: EST. OF TEODORO DEL ROSARIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/30/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The amended settlement agreement must be filed as an amendment to the petition and 15 day's notice must be given. This issue will require a hearing for judicial determination on how to proceed. 2) The supplement alleges funds are being relocated to ensure no more than $250k is mtainted in a single account. Please file bank statements showing how the assets are currently held. 3) Page 15 of the petition under prayer for relief does not state how the estate is to be distributed to each distribute, including the net amount of cash (as a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary. [See Local Rule 14.35 I. 6)] 4) Please provide a revised proposed order as #9 does not appear to be accurate as the property was sold. The proposed order does not have amounts on page 5 lines 12, and page 6 as required by Local rule 14.35 I. 6) 5) Please provide the legal authority for waiver of bond. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/15/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/7/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Petition for Final Distribution cannot be determined until the other matters are resolved. Current issues for Petition To Appoint Successor Personal Representative After Resignation Of Personal Representative; Declarations Of Alice Anivian And Summon Chouldjian In Support Thereof [Filed 03/30/2022 By Alice Anivian] 1. It appears this petition should be taken off calendar as it was not filed on the mandatory judicial council form DE-111. A petition on the mandatory judicial council was filed 4/14/2022. Current issues for Petition For Probate Of Will And For Letters Testamentary [Filed 04/14/2022 By Alice Anivian] 1. Must file proof of publication. 2. Must file Duties and Liabilities (DE-147). 3. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] Current issues for Petition For Probate Of Will And For Letters Of Administration And Letters Of Special Administration With General Powers [Filed 04/22/2022 By ARF Dashaksutyun, Western U.S.A. & Armenian Cultural Foundation] 1. It appears counsel did not comply with Local Rule 14.17 regarding getting the petition for special letters on the ex parte calendar. 2. Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of the petition and has not reviewed the entire petition so there may be additional issues. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3. Examiner does not have the required proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4. Petitioner did not select required boxes on page 2 under #5.a. Petitioner must select (3) or (4) and (7) or (8). 5. Notice must be re-done on all required parties including the request for special notice filed 4/11/2022. (Probate Code 1202). 6. Must file proof of publication. 7. Must file Duties and Liabilities (DE-147). 8. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/22/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CRAIG STEVEN PORTER SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The trustee requests fees of $6,880, based on one percent per annum of the ending value of the trust asset solely consisting of cash, for the trustee's services rendered during this two-year account period. The trustee's time records show only 16.5 hours of services actually provided. 2) The petitioner has not requested any fees for her services rendered as conservator on or after 02/01/2014. It is unclear whether the conservator is waiving fees for this trust accounting period or whether the conservator is deferring her fee request until the conservatorship is terminated. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SAMUEL V. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) The petition to determine heirship filed 06/04/2021 alleged Josephine V. Santos was still alive and was entitled to a 10% portion of the estate and the petition was verified by Eva Marie Santos on 6/2/2021. 11/19/2021 Supplemental Declaration of Eva Marie Santos page 2 lines 4-5 alleges Josephine V. Santos died 01/24/2020. No amendment changing this distribution was filed only Supplements and Declarations. No personal representative named. 01/31/2022 Declaration of Eva Marie Santos attached an affidavit translated by Eva Marie Santos. Unclear how this complies with California law. The 03/01/2022 and 03/28/2022 notice of hearings shows Josephine V. Santos was served after she died. 3) 01/31/2022 A "Sworn Statement (Affidavit)" translated by petitioner Eva Marie Santos was filed. Please file a translation by a disinterested party that is a professional. [LRSF 14.26 B.] Please explain the legal basis for how this alleged "written instrument" complies with California law. 4) Examiner requested petitioner give 15 day's notice to Michelangelo V. Santos. Notice of Hearing filed 07/07/2022 shows notice was emailed not sent to an address. 5) 02/28/2022 Declaration of Paul H. Melbostad alleges Peter Kekahuna survived Decedent and his estate is entitled to a share and he asked his relative to provide an address for the representative of his estate or other successor to his interest under the laws of the State of Hawaii. Unclear if there is a personal representative or issue. a. (See LRSF 14.67 I.) When an heir or beneficiary dies during the administration of an estate and survives any survival period stated in the will, the order must provide for distribution to the named personal representative of the estate of the heir or beneficiary (Probate Code 11801-11802) or, where applicable, to the person(s) entitled thereto under Probate Code 13100 or 13500. A certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days, the original 13100 affidavit, a copy of the acceptance of trusteeship, or a certified copy of the Spousal Property Order must be filed before the hearing date. 6) 02/28/2022 Declaration of Paul H. Melbostad alleges Lorna J. Gonzales post-deceased Decedent and died intestate and did not have a spouse or issue. Unclear why 03/01/2022 proof of services shows notice was given to the Estate of Loran J. Gonzales 1707 F Jimenez Apartment San Francisco Street Barangay Daniel Fajedo Las Pinas City, Metro Manila Philippines 1740.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AYANA NORWOOD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: There is no successor conservator of the ESTATE to whom assets can be transferred. Only a successor conservator of the person was appointed. How would a limited conservator of the person manage the remaining assets and the burial trust without any power to deal with them? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/3/22. Hopefully, a successor conservator of the estate can also be appointed Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST DARYL JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amendment was just filed on 7/21/22 (but not actually e-filed until 7/25/22, when it was available for viewing), which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. No proper proof of service of the notice of the amendment has been filed. A proof of service attached to the amendment is not sufficient. [CRC 7.53(a).] Furthermore, mailing notice before the filing of the pleading requiring notice is not sufficient notice. The annuity payments were also not true receipts includable in the fee base; they were principal payments that reduced the value of the annuity, not increased the value of the estate. There is no known e-filing charge of $65. E-filing costs should be no more than $5 per filed document. (Counsel might wish to change her procedures to give notice to the FTB as soon as the general personal representative is appointed, as the Probate Code requires the personal representative - not the petitioner - or her attorney to give such notice.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 10/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WOON FUNG CHAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Mailing notice BEFORE the filing of the pleading requiring notice is DEFECTIVE. Furthermore, although pleadings may be file stamped on the day they are uploaded, they are not effectively filed (available for review) until they are actually e-filed. The 4th amended petition was not e-filed until 7/5/22, on or after which date notice should have been mailed in order to be effective notice. (This issue was posted in earlier tentative rulings.) An amended petition completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3.] Any supplement or amendment to a prior petition is ineffective for a fourth amended petition. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
